name: Bleeding
about: Bleeding damage is repeated at the start of the affected creature's next turn.

name: Burning
about: A Burning creature take 1d6 damage at the start of each turn until they spend an Action to extinguish themselves.

name: Rotting
about: A rotting creature takes 1d6 damage at the start of each turn or once every ten minutes. This continues until they have rested or recieved any healing.

name: Poisoned
about: Poisoned creatures must make a inner fire check at the start of each turn or take one level of exhaustion and 1d6 damage.

name: Frenzied
about: A frenzied creature deals 1d6 bonus damage on all physical attacks and gains 15 feet of movement. Frenzied creatures can spend their reaction to force an enemy to roll an attack at disadvantage.

name: Guarded
about: Damage rolls against guarded creatures are at disadvantage.

name: Exposed
about: Damage rolls against exposed creatures are at advantage.

name: Weakened
about: A Weakened creature's attacks deal half damage.

name: Staggered
about: A Staggered creature makes their next action or damage roll at disadvantage.

name: Infused
about: An Infused object deals 1d6 bonus damage when used as a weapon.

name: Vulnerable
about: Damage rolls against vulnerable creatures are doubled.

name: Resistant
about: Damage rolls against resistant creatures are halved.

name: Invincible
about: Invincible creatures are immune to incoming damage.

name: Incapacitated
about: An Incapacitated creature cannot move or act and damage rolls made against them are at advantage.

name: Hardened
about: A Hardened creature's body has been rendered stonelike. They they are resistant to physical damage, but cannot move or act."

name: Halted
about: A halted creature immediately loses the remainder of their movement until the start of their next turn.

name: Spurred to Action
about: A creature that is Spurred to Action immediately gains 15 feet of movement and an action.

name: Hastened
about: A Hastened creature moves at twice its ordinary speed and gains one extra action on its turn. When a creature loses the hastened condition, they immediately take one level of Exhaustion unless otherwise stated.

name: Hindered
about: A Hindered creature loses half its movement.

name: Quickened
about: A Quickened creature gains 15 feet of movement and ignores difficult terrain.

name: Spurred to Movement
about: An entity that is Spurred to Movement immediately gains 15 feet of movement. The movement does not provoke reactions unless otherwise stated.

name: Prone
about: A prone creature moves at half speed, makes checks and attacks at disadvantage, and attacks against them are at advantage. They may spend an action to stand up.

name: Stunned
about: A Stunned creature loses either an action or offhand action on it's next turn (their choice).

name: Lured
about: A Lured creature immediately uses its full movement to go towards the object that is luring them. The creature maintains its intelligence and will act accordingly to avoid danger as they move. The movement does not provoke exposed attacks.

name: Tethered
about: A Tethered creature has been bound to something by a link. They cannot move further from the person or object than the maximum length of the tether. Either creature may make force a Strength Resistance Check to attempt to pull the other 15ft closer. The tethered creature may call for a repeated Resistance Check to attempt to break free.

name: Hard to Move
about: Creatures that are hard to move have advantage on any checks which could cause them to fall prone. If they are pushed, they move only half the intended distance.

name: Blinded
about: A Blinded creature fails all sight based checks, makes damage rolls at disadvantage, and must move at half speed.

name: Invisible
about: An Invisible creature cannot be seen, makes stealth checks at advantage, and attacks against them are at disadvantage.

name: Truesight
about: A creature with Truesight is able to see invisible objects and entities.

name: Darkvision
about: A creature with Darkvision is able to see up to 90ft even in pitch darkness. In low light, they are able to see as though it is day.

name: Charmed
about: A Charmed creature must act in a way that benefits its charmer and has disadvantage on checks made against them. If a charmed creature is harmed by its charmer or their allies, the effect immediately ends.

name: Controlled
about: A Controlled creature must fight for its controller and act as they instruct. If the controller or its allies harm a controlled creature, control ends.

name: Estranged
about: An Estranged creature acts as though everyone is its enemy.

name: Fixated
about: Fixated creatures are interested only in the object of their fixation. The nature of this fixation is left to the DM unless otherwise specified by a spell or ability.

name: Dumbstruck
about: A Dumbstruck creature is incapable of complex thought or speech. It cannot use spells or abilities and can communicate only in broken phrases. The creature retains its allegiances and instincts.

name: Blocked
about: A Blocked creature's magic has been weakened. If an adventurer is blocked, they must make an Inner Fire check against the Threat of the creature that is blocking them, and on failure the spell fails and is wasted. If a mosnter is blocked, they adventurer must make an Inner Fire against the creature's Resistance, and on success the monsters's spell fails and is wasted.

name: Muted
about: Muted creatures cannot make sound and cannot cast spells with verbal components.

name: Grappled
about: A grappled creature cannot move, and has disadvantage on Strength Checks, Dexterity Checks, and attacks. They may attempt to free themself as an action, repeating the check that caused the grapple.

name: Shrunk
about: A Shrunk creature is Tiny. Their movement speed is halved, and targeted ranged damage rolls against them are at disadvantage. They can move through enemy spaces and fit through small openings.

name: Enlarged
about: An Enlarged creature increases in size. They have advantage on Strength checks and their reach increases by 5ft. When they hit with a physical attack, they may choose to push their target 5ft away.

name: Ethereal
about: Ethereal creatures are visible but translucent and can pass through substances up to one inch thick. They cannot be affected by physical attacks, nor can they make attacks or cast spells.

name: Transformed
about: Transformed creatures magically take on the form and appearance of another creature. For the duration, they use the stats of that creature, including health, abilities, and stats. While transformed, a creature cannot use the abilities from it's original state, unless otherwise specified by the ability that granted the Transformed property. The transform ability instantly ends if the creature's health hits zero. Any carryover damage is taken by the creature's original form.

name: Diminished
about: A Diminished creature must subtract 2 from all checks.

name: Elusive
about: Elusive entities are immune to exposed attacks.

name: Isolated
about: An isolated creature cannot be affected by friendly magic or healing.

name: Fearless
about: Fearless creatures are immune to the Frightened condition.

name: Frightened
about: A Frightened creature cannot move closer to the source of its fear and must make damage rolls against it at disadvantage.

name: Harried
about: A Harried creature makes ranged damage rolls at disadvantage.

name: Immune
about: Immune creatures cannot be affected by status effects. Immune creatures cannot gain new status effects, but ongoing effects still apply unless otherwise stated.
